title: Cozy Filipino Herbal Flaky Tinola
description: A comforting Filipino dish made with ginger, chicken, and flaky plantains, all infused with the sweetness of condensed milk. This recipe is perfect for a cozy evening with family and friends.
instructions: Rinse the chicken breast and pat it dry with paper towels.
In a large skillet, heat the coconut oil over medium heat. Add the sliced ginger and cook until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the flaked plantain to the skillet and cook until it starts to brown, about 3 minutes.
Add the chicken breast to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, mix the condensed milk, fish sauce, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl.
Once the chicken is done, remove it from the oven and brush it with the condensed milk mixture.
Return the chicken to the oven and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Remove the chicken from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serve the chicken with the remaining plantain and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
